workflow.persona: Internal product strategist mapping the annual updating amendment workflow used by a compliance or operations team
workflow.entry_condition: The adviser is approaching its annual updating amendment deadline and needs to refresh Form ADV, brochure disclosures, and filing readiness materials.
workflow.completion_condition: The annual updating amendment is submitted, brochure and notice-filing follow-ups are queued or completed, and the filing package is archived.
workflow_notes: This repo still treats each workflow slice as a locked artifact, not a multi-workflow product surface.
This second slice stays inside RIA compliance but changes the work shape from pre-publication marketing review to periodic filing readiness.
Representative internal artifacts are included as workflow inputs, not as fabricated evidence.
Source evidence is limited to SEC instructions, staff guidance FAQs, risk alerts, and rulemaking releases.
Firm-specific routing, counsel involvement, and filing-calendar ownership are treated as local configuration, not unresolved scoring uncertainty.

sources.title: Form ADV - General Instructions
sources.summary: Explains the annual updating amendment deadline, full-form update requirement, brochure material-change filing, other-than-annual amendment triggers, and electronic brochure filing through IARD.

sources.title: Form ADV
sources.summary: States that Form ADV filing is mandatory, publicly available through IARD, required annually within 90 days of fiscal year end, and subject to federal liability for intentional misstatements or omissions.

sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions on Form ADV and IARD
sources.summary: Explains annual versus other-than-annual amendments, filing-fee funding, notice-filing mechanics, handling mistaken filing types, and when advisers must switch registration status after reporting ineligibility.

sources.title: Staff Responses to Questions About Part 2 of Form ADV
sources.summary: Explains how advisers should file and deliver updated brochures, including annual updating amendment brochure obligations, material-change disclosures, searchable PDF filing, and delivery timing to existing clients.

sources.title: The Five Most Frequent Compliance Topics Identified in OCIE Examinations of Investment Advisers
sources.summary: Highlights common adviser deficiencies including inaccurate or untimely regulatory filings, stale compliance manuals, and weak annual-review processes.

sources.title: Amendments to Form ADV
sources.summary: Summarizes the 2010 Form ADV Part 2 changes, including annual summary-of-material-change obligations, plain-English brochure disclosure topics, electronic brochure filing, and delivery expectations.

sources.title: SEC Adopts Reforms Relating to Investment Advisers Operating Exclusively Through the Internet
sources.summary: Explains the amended internet-adviser exemption, including the need to update Form ADV with an eligibility representation and the consequences if a firm no longer qualifies for SEC registration on that basis.

artifacts.name: Annual amendment input checklist
artifacts.description: Structured request for entity changes, office data, websites, social links, conflicts, disciplinary events, and other annual filing inputs.

artifacts.name: Prior Form ADV and brochure export
artifacts.description: The most recent filed Form ADV and brochure package used as the comparison baseline for the annual update.

artifacts.name: Current firm profile tracker
artifacts.description: Current legal-entity details, office roster, website and social-media list, and ownership or control changes since the last filing.

artifacts.name: Regulatory AUM and eligibility workpaper
artifacts.description: Calculation workbook and notes supporting SEC or state registration eligibility, internet-adviser status, and any switch or withdrawal implications.

artifacts.name: Brochure redline and summary of material changes
artifacts.description: Draft Part 2A updates, redlines, and the annual summary of material changes prepared for review and filing.

artifacts.name: Disciplinary and conflicts tracker
artifacts.description: Tracker of disciplinary events, conflicts, soft-dollar arrangements, compensation changes, and other disclosure-sensitive developments.

artifacts.name: State notice-filing and fee matrix
artifacts.description: State-by-state notice-filing requirements, IARD funding needs, and expected fees tied to the annual amendment cycle.

artifacts.name: Annual filing readiness memo
artifacts.description: Memo summarizing changes, unresolved issues, delivery obligations, and recommendation on whether the annual amendment is ready to file.

artifacts.name: IARD submission receipts and fee confirmations
artifacts.description: Accepted filing receipt, submission timestamp, fee confirmations, and any returned or corrected filing records.

artifacts.name: Delivery and retention log
artifacts.description: Evidence of brochure delivery or offer, retention metadata, and queued post-filing follow-up tasks.

steps.step_description: Collect the prior filing, current firm profile, annual input checklist, and eligibility workpapers into one normalized packet before any drafting begins.

automation_fit.rationale: The work is repeated, checklist-driven packet assembly with stable annual input categories.

human_signoff_need.rationale: A reviewer should inspect exceptions, but the packet-building step itself does not need final authority.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: Errors can still be caught downstream before filing, so the immediate downside is more about rework than irreversible harm.

evidence_strength.rationale: The annual packet can be anchored to explicit filing instructions, annual deadlines, and common examination deficiencies.

evidence_notes.claim: The annual updating amendment requires updates across all applicable form parts and schedules, which supports a standardized input packet.
The annual filing obligation remains separate from other-than-annual amendments, so assembling one clear annual packet reduces filing-type confusion.
Form ADV is a mandatory annual public filing, which makes complete packet assembly an accuracy-control step rather than optional admin work.
The SEC has flagged inaccurate or untimely regulatory filings and stale compliance materials as recurring deficiencies, which favors normalized packet assembly.

steps.build_implication: Build a structured intake lane that requests annual filing inputs, normalizes them into one packet, and highlights missing items before any reviewer time is spent.

steps.step_name: Reconcile registration basis and profile fields
steps.step_description: Compare current firm facts against Part 1A registration-basis, office, website, social-media, and eligibility fields before drafting the annual amendment.

automation_fit.rationale: Many fields are structured comparisons, but eligibility and profile-edge cases still need reviewer interpretation.

human_signoff_need.rationale: A reviewer should confirm registration-basis changes because they can affect SEC versus state status and public disclosure.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: A wrong eligibility or profile answer can create registration and disclosure problems that are expensive to unwind.

evidence_strength.rationale: The work can be tied to explicit instruction-level update triggers, FAQ guidance, and current SEC rule changes.

evidence_notes.claim: The instructions distinguish annual and prompt-update items, which makes the comparison set explicit but still leaves interpretation on material inaccuracies.
The FAQ explains that advisers reporting ineligibility on the annual amendment must switch registration within 180 days and that other-than-annual amendments do not replace the annual filing.
Internet advisers now must update Form ADV with an eligibility representation, which creates a live edge case for registration-basis review.
The SEC has identified inaccurate regulatory filings as a recurring deficiency, which supports keeping profile-field reconciliation in a reviewer-visible lane.

steps.build_implication: Build assistive comparison tooling that flags changed eligibility and profile fields, but keep the final registration-basis conclusion with a reviewer.

steps.step_description: Review Part 2A narrative disclosures, fee language, conflicts, disciplinary items, and summary of material changes against the firm's current practices.

automation_fit.rationale: The disclosure headings are structured, but deciding whether narrative disclosures fairly reflect current practice still requires human judgment.

human_signoff_need.rationale: A senior reviewer should confirm brochure and material-change language because it is client-facing and disclosure-sensitive.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: Misstated brochure disclosures can create examination and client-communication problems that outweigh the efficiency gain from blind automation.

evidence_strength.rationale: The brochure workflow is strongly anchored in Form ADV instructions and SEC Part 2 guidance, even though the writing remains judgment-heavy.

evidence_notes.claim: Annual updating amendments must refresh Part 2A and include the summary of material changes, which gives this review a clear procedural backbone.
The SEC staff explains that the brochure filed with the annual amendment also drives delivery timing to existing clients, which raises the importance of disclosure accuracy.
The guide ties the brochure to plain-English disclosure of fees, conflicts, disciplinary history, and business practices, which keeps this step reviewer-sensitive.
The required Part 2 topic structure means a tool can organize draft comparisons and redlines even though a reviewer still needs to judge the final wording.

steps.build_implication: Build redline and change-detection support for brochure sections, but keep final narrative judgment and disclosure calls in a reviewer-confirmed assist lane.

steps.step_name: Draft the annual updating amendment and notice checklist
steps.step_description: Prefill changed fields, assemble brochure attachments, and generate the annual updating amendment and notice-filing checklist for reviewer confirmation.

automation_fit.rationale: This is structured drafting and checklist generation tied to known items, schedules, and filing mechanics.

human_signoff_need.rationale: A reviewer should validate the staged filing, but the drafting and checklist work itself is largely repeatable.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: A bad prefill is still meaningful, but it can be caught before filing if the staged package remains reviewable.

evidence_strength.rationale: The step can be grounded in explicit form instructions, brochure-filing rules, fee mechanics, and public filing requirements.

evidence_notes.claim: The instructions define the parts and schedules that must be updated in the annual amendment, which makes prefill and checklist generation highly structured.
The FAQ explains that annual amendment fees and state notice fees must be funded in IARD before submission, which supports a deterministic filing checklist.
The Part 2 FAQ explains that advisers prepare their own brochure PDF and submit it through IARD, which fits staged document-generation support.
Because the filing becomes public and is used for regulatory purposes, the drafting lane should emphasize traceable staging rather than hidden automation.

steps.build_implication: Build the first product wedge here: structured field drafting, brochure attachment staging, and fee-aware filing checklists that remain reviewable before submission.

steps.step_name: Prepare the readiness memo and exception list
steps.step_description: Summarize changes, unresolved issues, brochure-delivery obligations, and escalation items into a memo that tells the signer whether the annual amendment is ready to file.
steps.human_owner_role: Compliance manager or outsourced compliance lead

automation_fit.rationale: The memo can be scaffolded from structured findings, but the significance and escalation framing still need human judgment.

human_signoff_need.rationale: A reviewer should own the final readiness recommendation because it synthesizes filing, disclosure, and exception judgment.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: A weak readiness memo can let a bad filing reach the signer, so the downside remains meaningfully higher than the speed gain.

evidence_strength.rationale: The memo can be anchored to explicit filing rules, exam observations, brochure-delivery obligations, and the public nature of the filing.

evidence_notes.claim: The instructions specify the annual and prompt-update obligations that the memo should roll up into a filing-readiness recommendation.
Recurring SEC findings around inaccurate filings and stale annual reviews support keeping the readiness memo explicit and reviewer-visible.
Brochure filing and delivery timing can create concrete post-filing obligations, so the memo should call out required follow-up rather than treat submission as the end of the job.
Because intentional misstatements or omissions can carry federal consequences, unresolved exceptions should be clearly surfaced to the accountable signer.

steps.build_implication: Build memo-drafting and issue-rollup support, but keep reviewer confirmation prominent because this step still packages judgment for the signer.

steps.step_name: Authorize and submit the annual amendment
steps.step_description: The accountable signer reviews the final package, confirms accuracy, authorizes fee-funded submission, and files the annual updating amendment through IARD.

automation_fit.rationale: Automation can stage information, but the act of certifying and submitting the filing should not be delegated.

human_signoff_need.rationale: This is the accountable approval and submission point for a regulatory filing.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: A wrong submission creates public, regulatory, and potentially enforcement-facing consequences with little upside from removing the signer.

evidence_strength.rationale: The human gate is strongly grounded in the form instructions, filing mechanics, and the SEC's statements about the filing's consequences.

evidence_notes.claim: Form ADV is mandatory, public, and subject to federal liability for intentional misstatements or omissions, which makes autonomous submission a poor fit.
Failure to update Form ADV as required can lead to registration consequences, which reinforces the need for a named accountable signer.
The FAQ ties submission to funded IARD accounts, filing type choices, and registration-status consequences, all of which are reviewer-signoff sensitive.
Even when a firm has filed interim amendments, the SEC still expects the distinct annual updating amendment, which makes the execution step a conscious filing decision rather than a background sync.

steps.build_implication: Keep execution and submission human. Product work here should stop at approval support, fee checks, and audit-ready packet assembly.

steps.step_name: Archive receipts, delivery evidence, and follow-ups
steps.step_description: Store filing receipts, brochure-delivery evidence, notice-filing confirmations, and queued follow-up tasks in the annual books-and-records package.

automation_fit.rationale: This is structured packaging, timestamp capture, and follow-up creation after the filing is complete, but firm-specific recordkeeping variations still matter.

human_signoff_need.rationale: A person may inspect exceptions, but the default recordkeeping and task-queue work is mechanical.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: The main risk is incomplete records or missed delivery tasks, which can usually be corrected if surfaced promptly.

evidence_strength.rationale: Archival and follow-up tasks are well anchored in brochure-delivery guidance, filing instructions, and SEC books-and-records expectations.

evidence_notes.claim: The annual amendment workflow includes brochure filing and update obligations, which supports structured capture of receipts and retained records after submission.
Updated brochures filed with the annual amendment can trigger delivery to existing clients, so post-filing follow-up should be systematically logged.
The SEC's brochure guidance ties annual material-change communication to delivery obligations, which makes delivery evidence part of the annual package rather than an afterthought.
The SEC has highlighted books-and-records and stale compliance processes as common deficiencies, which supports automating archival completeness checks and follow-up creation.

steps.build_implication: Build automated archival packaging, receipt capture, and follow-up task creation so the filing cycle ends with a clean books-and-records bundle instead of manual cleanup.
